title: Anguina trifoliata
intro: Unfortunately, I am unable to verify any information about Anguina trifoliata.
taxonomy: Anguina trifoliata is a type of nematode, which belongs to the phylum Nematoda and the class Secernentea. It is further classified as a member of the order Tylenchida and the family Anguinidae. The genus Anguina also includes several other species of nematodes. Within the plant parasitic nematodes, Anguina trifoliata is considered a root-feeding nematode. This classification places it among the parasitic nematodes that feed on plant roots and other below-ground plant tissues.
benefits: I couldn't find any information on the health benefits of Anguina trifoliata.
